question: At the Hardy house there was great consternation when Aunt Gertrude saw Joe helping Frank up the stairs.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Surprise : an unexpected or astonishing event, fact, etc. Panic : sudden uncontrollable fear or anxiety, often causing wildly unthinking behaviour. Gaiety : the state or quality of being light-hearted or cheerful. Satisfaction : fulfilment of one's wishes, expectations, or needs, or the pleasure derived from this. Consternation : a feeling of anxiety or dismay, typically at something unexpected. Synonym of Consternation is Surprise

question: He murdered the man without any qualms of conscience.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Hesitation : the action of pausing before saying or doing something. Pang : a sudden sharp pain or painful emotion. Excitement : a feeling of great enthusiasm and eagerness. Curiosity : a strong desire to know or learn something. Qualms : an uneasy feeling of doubt, worry, or fear, especially about one's own conduct; a misgiving. Synonym of Qualms is Hesitation

question: He did not make any attempt to placate her.

Serve : perform duties or services for (another person or an organization). Satisfy : meet the expectations, needs, or desires of (someone). Placate : make (someone) less angry or hostile. Synonym of Placate is Pacify

question: The traditional methods of doing things have now become obsolescent.

question: It is not for lay readers to evaluate a great poet.

Lay : not having professional qualifications or expert knowledge, especially in law or medicine. Synonym of Lay is Ordinary

question: It was on the behest of the officer that he made those improvements.

Orders : the arrangement or disposition of people or things in relation to each other according to a particular sequence, pattern, or method. Behest : a person's orders or command. Synonym of Behest is Orders

question: He has won great admiration amongst his students because of his verdant outlook.

question: Those who man government-run shops also have their palm greased.

Rule : one of a set of explicit or understood regulations or principles governing conduct or procedure within a particular area of activity. Man : (of personnel) work at, run, or operate (a place or piece of equipment) or defend (a fortification). Synonym of Man is Operate

question: Instead of being arraigned in public, the child should be quietly interviewed in public.

question: The man lived in a dilapidated house made of mud and bricks.

question: In his lust for wealth, he had reached the zenith of criminal activity.

Discover : find unexpectedly or during a search. Prove : demonstrate the truth or existence of (something) by evidence or argument. Ascertain : find (something) out for certain; make sure of. Synonym of Ascertain is Determine

question: The need of the hour is to initiate the renaissance of moral conscience and halt the rampant corruption, for it could even hasten the end of our civilisation.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Rise : move from a lower position to a higher one; come or go up. Introduction : the action of introducing something. Revival : an improvement in the condition, strength, or fortunes of someone or something. Significance : the quality of being worthy of attention; importance. Renaissance : a revival of or renewed interest in something. Synonym of Renaissance is Revival

question: The newly found drug on the epidemic was not at all deleterious.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Effective : successful in producing a desired or intended result. Intoxicating : (of alcoholic drink or a drug) liable to cause someone to lose control of their faculties or behaviour. Noxious : harmful, poisonous, or very unpleasant. Successful : accomplishing a desired aim or result. Deleterious : causing harm or damage. Synonym of Deleterious is Noxious

question: That case is not amenable to ordinary rules.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Susceptible : capable or admitting of. Interpreted : explain the meaning of (information or actions). Contradictory : mutually opposed or inconsistent. Dealt : distribute (cards) in an orderly rotation to players for a game or round. Amenable : capable of being acted upon in a particular way; susceptible. Synonym of Amenable is Susceptible

question: Hearing the news, the workers were in a state of euphoria.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Elation : great happiness and exhilaration. Indignation : anger or annoyance provoked by what is perceived as unfair treatment. Depression : feelings of severe despondency and dejection. Disorder : a state of confusion. Euphoria : a feeling or state of intense excitement and happiness. Synonym of Euphoria is Elation

question: He was ingenious enough to know how to get out of the clutches of law.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Clever : quick to understand, learn, and devise or apply ideas; intelligent. Intelligent : having or showing intelligence, especially of a high level. Alert : quick to notice any unusual and potentially dangerous or difficult circumstances; vigilant. Cunning : having or showing skill in achieving one's ends by deceit or evasion. Ingenious : (of a person) clever, original, and inventive. Synonym of Ingenious is Clever

question: His adulatory remarks surprised us.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Discreet : careful and prudent in one's speech or actions, especially in order to keep something confidential or to avoid embarrassment. Insulting : disrespectful or scornfully abusive. Appreciate : be grateful for (something). Funny : causing laughter or amusement; humorous. Adulatory : excessively praising or admiring. Synonym of Adulatory is Appreciate

question: Dr.S.Radhakrishnan has written an erudite commentary on the Bhagwad Gita.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Scholarly : involving or relating to serious academic study. Inspiring : having the effect of inspiring someone. Effective : successful in producing a desired or intended result. Perfect : having all the required or desirable elements, qualities, or characteristics; as good as it is possible to be. Erudite : having or showing great knowledge or learning. Synonym of Erudite is Scholarly

question: In the company of smart and crafty fellows, a gaby finds himself out of place.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Talkative : fond of or given to talking. Innocent : not guilty of a crime or offence. Honest : free of deceit; truthful and sincere. Cunning : having or showing skill in achieving one's ends by deceit or evasion. Gabby : excessively or annoyingly talkative. Synonym of Gabby is Talkative

question: He kept his eyes peeled and his ears pricked for some important clue.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Hint : a slight or indirect indication or suggestion. Notable : worthy of attention or notice; remarkable. Remarkable : worthy of attention; striking. Sensational : causing great public interest and excitement. Clue : a piece of evidence or information used in the detection of a crime. Synonym of Clue is Hint

question: AIDS is a chronic disease.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Incurable : (of a sick person or a disease) not able to be cured. infectious : (of a disease or disease-causing organism) liable to be transmitted to people, organisms, etc. through the environment. Contagious : (of a disease) spread from one person or organism to another, typically by direct contact. Persistent : continuing to exist or occur over a prolonged period. Chronic : (of an illness) persisting for a long time or constantly recurring. Synonym of Chronic is Persistent

question: These recent developments seem to be quite adverse to our interests.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Favourable : expressing approval. Indifferent : having no particular interest or sympathy; unconcerned. Unfortunate : having or marked by bad fortune; unlucky. Opposed : anxious to prevent or put an end to; disagreeing with. Adverse : preventing success or development; harmful; unfavourable. Synonym of Adverse is Opposed

question: The demand for an autonomous state is responsible for insurgence of terrorism in Punjab.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Separate : forming or viewed as a unit apart or by itself. Independent : free from outside control; not subject to another's authority. Secluded : (of a place) not seen or visited by many people; sheltered and private. Self-governed : (of a region or organization) having the freedom to govern itself or control its own affairs. Autonomous : having the freedom to act independently. Synonym of Autonomous is Self-governed

question: His impertinence and his salacious remarks spoke voliably of his degradation.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Rude : offensively impolite or bad-mannered. Indecent : not conforming with generally accepted standards of behaviour, especially in relation to sexual matters. Distraught : very worried and upset. Poor : lacking sufficient money to live at a standard considered comfortable or normal in a society. Salacious : having or conveying undue or inappropriate interest in sexual matters. Synonym of Salacious is Indecent

question: The value of some shares has appreciated considerably after the last budget.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Changed : make or become different. Increased : become or make greater in size, amount, or degree. Decreased : make or become smaller or fewer in size, amount, intensity, or degree. Crashed : (of a vehicle) collide violently with an obstacle or another vehicle. Appreciated : rise in value or price. Synonym of Appreciated is Increased

question: The quiz game comprises three rounds.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option B Solution: Continue : persist in an activity or process. Incorporates : take in or contain (something) as part of a whole; include. Retains : not abolish or alter; maintain. Maintain : cause or enable (a condition or situation) to continue. Comprises : consist of; be made up of. Synonym of Comprises is Incorporates

question: Peace keeping for the United Nations is, at best of times and arduous task.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Laborious : requiring considerable time and effort. Inconvenient : causing trouble, difficulties, or discomfort. Difficult : needing much effort or skill to accomplish, deal with, or understand. Delicate : very fine in texture or structure; of intricate workmanship or quality. Arduous : involving or requiring strenuous effort; difficult and tiring. Synonym of Arduous is Difficult

question: I realised they wanted to be alone together, so I felt very awkward.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option C Solution: Cumbersome : large or heavy and therefore difficult to carry or use; unwieldy. Unwieldy : (of an object) difficult to move because of its size, shape, or weight. Embarrassed : feeling or showing embarrassment. Clumsy : difficult to handle or use; unwieldy. Awkward : causing or feeling uneasy embarrassment or inconvenience. Synonym of Awkward is Embarrassed

question: We heard him asseverate his good intentions but his actions belied his words.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Affirm : state emphatically or publicly. Incite : encourage or stir up (violent or unlawful behaviour). Defy : openly resist or refuse to obey. Cancel : decide or announce that (a planned event) will not take place. Asseverate : declare or state solemnly or emphatically. Synonym of Asseverate is Affirm

question: For quick promotion, he hit on a plan to propitiate the Manager.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option D Solution: Evict : expel (someone) from a property, especially with the support of the law. Incite : encourage or stir up (violent or unlawful behaviour). Praise : express warm approval or admiration of. Conciliate : stop (someone) being angry or discontented; placate. Propitiate : win or regain the favour of (a god, spirit, or person) by doing something that pleases them. Synonym of Propitiate is Conciliate

question: There was nothing casual about my father's interests.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Ordinary : with no special or distinctive features; normal. Incidental : happening as a minor accompaniment to something else. Funny : causing laughter or amusement; humorous. Accidental : happening by chance, unintentionally, or unexpectedly. Casual : happening by chance; accidental. Synonym of Casual is Accidental

question: His grand parents had grown feeble and helpless.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Weak : lacking the power to perform physically demanding tasks; having little physical strength or energy. Incapacitated : deprived of strength or power; debilitated. Timid : showing a lack of courage or confidence; easily frightened. Humiliated : make (someone) feel ashamed and foolish by injuring their dignity and pride. Feeble : lacking physical strength, especially as a result of age or illness. Synonym of Feeble is Weak

question: My philosophy lecturer is unfailingly bombastic.
solution: Answer & Solution Answer: Option A Solution: Pompous : affectedly grand, solemn, or self-important. Impressive : evoking admiration through size, quality, or skill; grand, imposing, or awesome. Impressionistic : based on subjective reactions presented unsystematically. Sensitive : quick to detect or respond to slight changes, signals, or influences. Bombastic : high-sounding but with little meaning; inflated. Synonym of Bombastic is Pompous
